fanout.title: Fanout-on-Write Newsfeed
steps: User posts tweet → Django creates Tweet row
Signal fires → fanout_newsfeed_task.delay(tweet_id) sent to Celery via Redis broker
Celery worker fetches followers from Memcached (FriendshipService.get_followers)
bulk_create() inserts Newsfeed rows for author + all followers (unique_together prevents duplicates on retry)
Worker manually pushes each Newsfeed to Redis list (bulk_create skips post_save signals)
Feed request → Redis list served in O(1), falls back to DB only when cache is cold or exhausted
tradeoffs: Fan-out on write means read is O(1) — ideal for read-heavy feeds
Celery task retries with exponential backoff (max 3) — idempotent via unique_together
Redis list capped at 1000 entries per user; older items served from DB

caching.title: Two-Tier Caching

tiers.name: Object cache (Memcached in dev / Redis db2 in prod)
List cache (Redis db0)
tiers.stores: Follower lists, following sets, newsfeed lists, user tweet lists
tiers.invalidation: Signal handlers on follow/unfollow; manual push on new tweet

caching.devProdNote: Memcached is dev-only — Railway has no managed Memcached. In prod, a second Redis logical DB (db2) replaces it. The /architecture page reflects the prod topology.

pagination.title: Cursor Pagination
pagination.strategy: Opaque base64-encoded created_at timestamp — stable under concurrent inserts, no page-drift.

params.created_at__gt: ISO datetime — pull-to-refresh (new items since last seen)

pagination.whyNotOffset: Offset pagination drifts when new items are inserted during scroll — cursor encodes position in time, not row count.

n1fixes.title: N+1 Query Elimination
n1fixes.before: 20 tweets × 3 relations (likes, comments, photos) = 60+ queries per feed page
n1fixes.after: prefetch_related_objects(tweets, 'likes', 'comment_set', 'tweetphoto_set') → 3 batched queries regardless of page size
notes: GenericRelation 'likes' added to Tweet model so Django ORM can batch-fetch likes
User objects served from Memcached (cached_user property) — no DB join needed
select_related not needed for user because Memcached is cheaper than a JOIN for this access pattern

auth.title: JWT Authentication
auth.accessToken: 60-minute lifetime, stored in React AuthContext (module-level memory, clears on reload)
auth.refreshToken: 7-day lifetime, stored in httpOnly; SameSite=None; Secure cookie on the Next.js domain
auth.refreshFlow: Client → POST /api/auth/refresh (Next.js route handler, same-origin) → handler reads httpOnly cookie → proxies to Django /api/token/refresh/ → rotates cookie → returns new access token
auth.blacklist: Logout calls /api/auth/logout → handler reads cookie → blacklists token on Django → deletes cookie
